personas.persona: David Jones founded Torchlake after twenty years running plant engineering for an industrial operator, and he still writes like a plant guy: findings first, causes second, niceties never. He signs every engagement letter, reads every field report before it leaves, and strikes any sentence a facilities director could not act on.

Jennifer Mayo, Jen to everyone including clients by the second site visit, is the firm's Principal Engineer and its technical conscience. She reviews sequences of operations the way editors read proofs, and her margin notes are famous for the single word 'why?'. Writes sparingly but precisely; when Jen commits a finding to paper it has already been checked twice.

Jeff Fowler runs delivery as Engineering Manager and owns the kickoff memo and status report cadence. He thinks in checklists and owner-by-owner tables, keeps the schedule honest, and always names which Fowler he means since his brother-in-arms confusion with David Fowler is a running office joke. Businesslike on the page, dry in person.

John Johnson, Project Engineer, is the firm's default minute-taker and deficiency-list keeper. He records meetings nearly verbatim, tags every action with an owner and a due date, and follows up without being reminded. His prose is flat by design; he saves opinions for the drive back from site.

David Fowler, Staff Engineer, is happiest on a lift checking damper linkages and happiest second writing up exactly what he found. Field notes read like lab notebooks: time-stamped, unit-labeled, no adjectives. Newer to report writing, so his sections get a pass from Jeff before anything goes to a client.

Megan Thomas leads field operations: site scheduling, safety plans, instrument calibration logs, and the firm's books besides. She keeps the file share navigable and the financial summaries reconciled to the penny, and her emails are two sentences long because that is all a scheduling question needs.
